_meta.task: B.3 Medicaid ambulance rate card, footprint states
_meta.unit_note: All rates USD per one-way base transport unless unit says per mile
_meta.medicare_comparator: CY2026 AFS national unadjusted base (Derived_Rate_Card col D: RVU x CF $284.56; A0425 $9.15/mile)

A0426.notes: ALS non-emergency; same rate as A0427/A0433/A0434 (flat ALS rate)

NE.effective: 1 Jul 2026 (fee schedule 471-000-504, Ambulance Services, July 1 2026); schedule cover states rates carry NO increase vs prior year - no rate increase appropriations this state fiscal year
NE.pa_rules: PA column on the schedule is blank for every ground ambulance code (no prior-authorization flag); A0888/A0999 are BR (by report). Nebraska Medicaid ambulance policy is 471 NAC 4.
NE.broker_carve: YES (managed care). Heritage Health MCOs carve NEMT to transportation brokers: MTM Inc. for Nebraska Total Care and Molina, ModivCare for UnitedHealthcare with MTM Health taking over 1 Jan 2026 (UHCprovider notice). Residual FFS members get NET arranged by DHHS Medicaid transportation staff under the separate 471-000-503 NET fee schedule (1 Jan 2026 revision).
NE.dialysis_policy: No dialysis-specific restriction or rate row printed on the ambulance schedule; scheduled dialysis travel routes through the NET benefit (MCO broker or DHHS FFS NET). Not stated at code level in the retrieved schedule.
NE.retrieval: Direct download of the July 2026 xlsx from the DHHS Medicaid Provider Rates and Fee Schedules page; parsed with openpyxl. File title row: NEBRASKA MEDICAID FEE SCHEDULE, AMBULANCE SERVICES JULY 1, 2026 / 471-000-504.

IA.effective: Current portal extract accessed 11 Jul 2026; per-code effective dates 2014-2020 with open end dates (99999999). Public GEMT providers get a separate cost-based supplemental (Iowa GEMT program).
IA.pa_rules: No PA flag; non-emergency ambulance requires medical-necessity criteria - bed confinement before and after the trip, or restraint risk, ongoing IV, oxygen without portable system, or isolation (Ambulance Services Provider Manual, Ch. III, dated 1 Oct 2015).
IA.broker_carve: YES. NEMT broker for the three IA Health Link MCOs was Access2Care, renamed to MTM Health effective 18 Sep 2025; MTM also administers FFS NEMT (hhs.iowa.gov Medical Transportation page).
IA.dialysis_policy: NEMT rides for dialysis, chemo, or radiation can be scheduled up to 90 days in advance (member NEMT policy); ambulance claims carry destination modifiers G (hospital-based dialysis facility) and J (non-hospital dialysis facility) per the provider manual.
IA.retrieval: Interactive fee-schedule portal (I Accept gate on hhs.iowa.gov fee-schedules page -> XML index -> provider type 11 AMBULANCE -> C11.csv). Values read from the CSV: code, description, Factor column.

A0425.notes: Ambulance rate-type schedule, eff 07/01/2023; the general Medicaid-rate schedule still carries a legacy $3.00 row eff 07/01/2022

KS.effective: Current KMAP files posted with version date 7/1/2026 (FeeSchedule_20260701_TXIX_AMB.txt and FeeSchedule_20260701_TXIX_DEF.txt); ambulance code rates effective 7/1/2023 (the SFY2024 ambulance rebase).
KS.pa_rules: No PA, but ALL nonemergent transportation requires medical necessity and a Medical Necessity (MN) form attached to the claim at submission (KMAP professional/ambulance manual guidance).
KS.broker_carve: YES (managed care). KanCare is near-total managed care; NEMT runs through the MCO broker ModivCare (Kansas NEMT provider manual), which coordinates taxi, van, wheelchair, stretcher van and ambulance NEMT.
KS.dialysis_policy: Dialysis is named among critical services eligible for NEMT in the Kansas NEMT provider manual (ModivCare, May 2022).
KS.retrieval: KMAP public portal defeats simple fetch (Kendo grid + session). Replicated the AJAX flow with a session cookie jar: GetFeeScheduleProgram -> GetFeeScheduleRateType?program=TXIX -> POST FeeSheduleSearchCurrentResultList (program=TXIX, ratetype=AMB / DEF) -> GET DownloadFSFile?arg1=<zip> with referer. Files parsed as fixed-width text.

A0426.notes: HH (hospital-to-hospital) modifier only; base code noncovered; eff 07/01/2019

A0428.notes: HH and HD (hospital-to-diagnostic) modifiers only; base code noncovered; eff 07/01/2019

A0434.notes: NONCOVERED under MO HealthNet FFS (fee schedule row, since 07/01/2002)

MO.effective: Fee schedule file generated 7 Jul 2026 (provider files updated 07/08/2026); per-row rate effective dates 2019-2024 as noted.
MO.pa_rules: Prior Authorization column is No on every covered ambulance row. A0428 is billable ONLY for hospital-to-hospital transfer (HH) or hospital-to-diagnostic/therapeutic site (HD) per the MHD hot tip 'Ambulance Billing for Procedure Code A0428'.
MO.broker_carve: YES. Statewide NEMT broker is MTM (mydss.mo.gov/mhd/nemt: 'To become a NEMT provider, contact MTM'). Scheduled non-emergency transports outside the HH/HD ambulance contexts route through the broker, not the FFS schedule.
MO.dialysis_policy: Dialysis transports are not billable as FFS A0428 (HH/HD contexts only); scheduled dialysis travel is a broker (MTM) trip under the NEMT program.
MO.retrieval: Direct download from the MHD fee schedule downloads page (apps.dss.mo.gov/fmsfeeschedules/DLFiles.aspx); parsed with openpyxl.

OH.effective: 1 Jan 2024 (appendix to OAC rule 5160-15-28, appendix file dated 22 Dec 2023; rule effective 1 Jan 2024).
OH.pa_rules: No prior authorization in rule; non-emergency ground ambulance requires necessity per OAC 5160-15-23(B): EMT treatment/supervision, oxygen administration, or supervised protective restraint during transport, or a qualifying hospital-to-hospital transfer.
OH.broker_carve: PARTIAL / NO STATEWIDE BROKER. Non-ambulance NET runs through county CDJFS programs (OAC 5160-15-10) and MCO transportation benefits; ambulance remains a direct ODM FFS or MCO claim. Public EMS providers also receive a GEMT supplemental payment (OAC 5160-15-30, eff 7 Apr 2025) up to the Medicare payment ceiling.
OH.dialysis_policy: Rules retrieved are silent on dialysis-specific transport; no dialysis rate row in the appendix.
OH.retrieval: Direct download of the appendix PDF linked from codes.ohio.gov rule 5160-15-28; parsed with pdftotext.

WI.effective: Every ground code carries effective date 07/01/2008 on the live max-fee report (accessed 11 Jul 2026) - the schedule has not moved in 18 years.
WI.pa_rules: No code-level PA flag on the schedule (PA listed among general limitations). Non-emergency ambulance eligibility requires life support services (ALS/BLS) or supine transport; when NEMT-by-ambulance is coordinated through the NEMT manager the provider must obtain a trip ID within two business days and submit the claim to the manager (ForwardHealth Update 2014-32).
WI.broker_carve: YES. Statewide NEMT manager (MTM Inc.; Veyo held the contract from 1 Nov 2021 until MTM's acquisition; DHS NEMT pages now point to the MTM Link portal). Scheduled NEMT ambulance claims coordinated through the manager are paid by the manager, not ForwardHealth; FFS pays emergencies and NEMT not coordinated through the manager.
WI.dialysis_policy: Recurring-treatment rides (dialysis) are scheduled as standing rides through the NEMT manager; the ambulance max-fee schedule itself is silent on dialysis. Stretcher vans are a separate SMV max-fee schedule.
WI.retrieval: Direct download of the ForwardHealth portal-generated AMBULANCE MAXIMUM ALLOWABLE FEE SCHEDULE PDF; parsed with pdftotext.

A0425.notes: GROUND MILEAGE; eff 04/01/2026 (other ground codes eff 01/01/2026); factor code B

A0428.notes: BLS non-emergency; highest A0428 base in the footprint - coincidentally equals the CY2026 Medicare ambulance conversion factor ($284.56)

A0434.notes: Specialty Care Transport; highest SCT in the footprint

MN.effective: Rates effective 01/01/2026 (ground mileage A0425 effective 04/01/2026); MHCP Fee Schedule xlsx downloaded 12 Jul 2026. One max-fee row per ground code (factor code B), status A (active).
MN.pa_rules: No PA flag on the ambulance rows of the MHCP fee schedule. MHCP covers emergency and non-emergency ground and air ambulance, billed directly to MHCP by enrolled ambulance providers (ambulance is NOT routed through the administered NEMT system). Non-ambulance NEMT modes (Mode 1-4) must be billed through the administered system.
MN.broker_carve: MIXED / STATE-ADMINISTERED (no single statewide MCO broker). NEMT is split between local county/tribal agency-administered NEMT and State-Administered NEMT (administered by Acentra Health / Kepro). Ambulance is billed FFS by enrolled providers; the non-ambulance NEMT modes route through the administered system. NEMT providers must hold MnDOT Special Transportation Service (STS) certification.
MN.dialysis_policy: Explicit return-leg rule: for dialysis members eligible for local county/tribal NEMT, State-Administered NEMT may cover the RETURN trip; Kepro/Acentra enters a single-day state-administered NEMT certification for dialysis return trips only, unless state-administered NEMT is determined necessary at all times.
MN.retrieval: Direct download of the MHCP Fee Schedule xlsx (Sheet1, 48,924 rows) from mn.gov/dhs; parsed with openpyxl. Ground rows A0425-A0434 read from the Max Fee column; effective dates 01/01/2026 (A0425 04/01/2026), status A.

A0425.notes: GROUND MILEAGE (TRANS service category); reduced-modifier variants (U3/U5, MRT/SE) pay $1.67; eff 01/01/2026

IN.effective: Rates effective 01/01/2026, updated to reflect 100% of the CY2025 Medicare fee schedule per IHCP bulletin BT2025156; IHCP Professional Fee Schedule xlsx last updated 9 Jun 2026 (changes through 5/26/2026). Prior-year (2025) rate rows carried for DOS through 12/31/2025 (e.g. A0428 $262.72 in 2025 -> $269.02 in 2026).
IN.pa_rules: No PA flag on the covered ambulance rows (PA column blank). A0434 SCT is Non-Covered (PA=Y, $0.00). ALS/BLS ambulance is EXEMPT from the FFS NEMT brokerage requirement and arranged directly with the transportation provider (billed FFS).
IN.broker_carve: PARTIAL. FFS NEMT for non-ambulance modes (common carrier, taxi, wheelchair van) is brokered statewide through Verida (formerly Southeastrans); managed-care members go through their MCE. ALS/BLS ambulance is carved OUT of the broker requirement and billed FFS directly, so the ambulance base rates are the real FFS price.
IN.dialysis_policy: Recurring dialysis trips are arranged as standing orders through the Verida FFS broker (or the MCE for managed-care members); the ambulance fee schedule itself is silent on dialysis at code level.
IN.retrieval: IHCP maxfee portal (agreement gate): GET maxfee_validate.asp?agreement=accept to set the session cookie, then fee_home.asp -> download 'Professional Fee Schedule.xlsx' (37,422 rows) in-session; parsed with openpyxl. Ground rows A0425-A0434, Max Fee/Non-Facility column, Rate Effective Date 01/01/2026. Policy confirmed in bulletin BT2025156 (in.gov/medicaid).

A0425.notes: BLS mileage (A0425 UB) Other $2.50 / Hospital $3.00; ALS mileage (A0425 UA) Other $2.50 / Hospital $4.00; non-emergency stretcher mileage $2.00; return-trip mileage $2.00

A0426.notes: NOT LISTED on the KY transportation fee schedule (no ALS1 non-emergency line)

A0427.notes: ALS1 emergency BASE RATE, Other (independent) $60.00 / Hospital-based $110.00; additional-patient A0427 GM $25.00

A0428.notes: NON-EMERGENCY STRETCHER base rate (Provider Type 56, Specialty 16), flat $55.00 - a stretcher-van line, not a full BLS ambulance; formerly billed as T2005 until renamed A0428 eff 01/01/2025; additional-patient $10.00

A0429.notes: BLS emergency BASE RATE, Other (independent) $60.00 / Hospital-based $82.50; additional-patient A0429 GM $20.00; medical first response A0429 UC $110.00

A0433.notes: NOT LISTED on the KY transportation fee schedule (no ALS2 line)

A0434.notes: NOT LISTED on the KY transportation fee schedule (no SCT line)

KY.effective: KY Medicaid Transportation Fee Schedule 2026, revised 1/9/2026 (schedule update log: 'No changes or updates to the 2026 fee schedule'). Provider Type 55 = emergency ambulance; Provider Type 56 Specialty 16 = non-emergency stretcher. Rates carry Hospital vs Other (independent provider) destination variants; the 'Other' rate is recorded as headline.
KY.pa_rules: Schedule does not flag PA at code level. Emergency ambulance (PT55) billed FFS; the only non-emergency ambulance line is the PT56 non-emergency stretcher (A0428). Broader NEMT is arranged through the HSTD regional broker and not billed on this schedule.
KY.broker_carve: YES. NEMT runs through the Human Service Transportation Delivery (HSTD) program - DMS contracts with the Kentucky Transportation Cabinet (KYTC), which assigns a regional broker to each county; providers contract with the broker and normal trips must be booked 72 hours ahead. The five KY Medicaid MCOs administer NEMT for their members.
KY.dialysis_policy: Recurring dialysis trips route through the HSTD regional broker (or the member's MCO); the ambulance/stretcher fee schedule is silent on dialysis at code level.
KY.retrieval: Direct download (browser UA + referer) of the CHFS DMS 2026 Transportation Rates PDF; parsed with pdftotext -layout. Rates read from the 'Reimbursement Rate & Destination in parenthesis' column.

A0425.notes: PENDING current - 2009 crossover table has A0425 built into the sliding scale (see A0427/A0429 notes); no current-effective standalone mileage rate on a public file

A0426.notes: PENDING current - appears only on the 2009-2012 crossover rate table 'for crossover calculation only'; no current standalone FFS rate published

A0427.notes: PENDING current. VERIFIED STALE 2009 sliding scale (DMAS Emergency Ground Ambulance PDF, 'Rates Effective with Dates of Service October 31, 2009 and Before'): 1-5 mi = $75.00 total, 6-10 mi = $150.00 total, then $150 plus $2.50 per mile for 11+ miles. Managed-care enrollees are paid by their MCO, not this file.

A0428.notes: PENDING - not payable on a public FFS schedule: must be preauthorized and billed to the FFS NEMT broker (DMAS Transportation manual Ch.5, rev 12/5/2025: 'A0428 Non-Emergency Basic Life Support Ambulance - These transports need to be preauthorized and billed to the Non-Emergency Medicaid Transportation Broker (866) 386-8331')

A0429.notes: PENDING current. Same verified STALE 2009 sliding scale as A0427 (1-5 mi $75 / 6-10 mi $150 / +$2.50 per mile after 10).

A0433.notes: PENDING current - crossover-only on the 2009-2012 table; no current standalone FFS rate published

A0434.notes: PENDING current - crossover-only on the 2009-2012 table; no current standalone FFS rate published

VA.effective: PARKED for current dollar rates. VERIFIED 12 Jul 2026: the two public DMAS ambulance rate files are both stale - the Emergency Ground Ambulance PDF (A0427/A0429) is captioned 'Rates Effective with Dates of Service October 31, 2009 and Before', and the crossover rate table (A0425/A0426/A0427/A0428/A0429/A0433/A0434) is captioned 'For all Dates of Service November 1, 2009 to June 30, 2012' and is for Medicare crossover calculation only. No current-effective standalone FFS ambulance fee file is published; the DMAS procedure fee file search portal (interactive per-code lookup) would fill the current rate.
VA.pa_rules: Non-emergency ambulance (A0426/A0428) requires broker preauthorization BEFORE transport; emergency codes (A0427/A0429) billed FFS without broker contact.
VA.broker_carve: YES - the strongest carve in the set: FFS non-emergency ambulance itself is billed to the DMAS NEMT broker, so no public FFS price exists for scheduled ambulance transport.
VA.dialysis_policy: Scheduled dialysis transport is a broker trip (all FFS NEMT service types must be arranged and confirmed by the broker before transport).
VA.retrieval: Both DMAS ambulance rate PDFs fetched (browser UA + referer) and verified with pdftotext -layout: emergency ground (DOS Oct 31 2009 and before) and crossover table (DOS Nov 1 2009 - Jun 30 2012). Transportation manual Ch.5 (rev 12/5/2025) confirms the A0428 broker carve. Current per-code FFS rates PARKED as PENDING with the DMAS procedure fee file portal named as the filler dataset.
